headers.//: No-cache fallback for every request path (SPA routes like `/`, `/today`, `/check-in` all resolve to index.html via the rewrite above, but Firebase matches header rules against the REQUEST path — not the rewritten path — so we need a wildcard here). The next rule pins hashed asset URLs to immutable, overriding this for /assets/**.

Hashed asset filenames change on every rebuild, so they're safe to cache forever. This rule comes LAST — the last matching header wins for the same key, so /assets/** gets immutable instead of the wildcard's no-cache.
Icons are hashed by name too and rarely change — cache them a week.
